### Post by Spr0k3t on 2007-10-01
**This post could be related to an Ubuntu bug filed at**: [https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-restricted-modules-2.6.22/+bug/144777](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-restricted-modules-2.6.22/+bug/144777) [br].[/br] ---------------------------- [br].[/br] [br].[/br]
				I was able to get it working... but not without pulling some hair out.  So the ultimate question is how is this attainable without the loss of hair.

First things first, make a backup of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf file. Install the nvidia-glx drivers (or nvidia-glx-new if you have a 7 series or newer card).  Go to the restricted drivers manager and enable the card.  Then you will need to open the nvidia settings```
gksudo nvidia-settings
```with admin rights.  On the second option, you will notice that "Xinerama" is enabled.  Disable this option.  Select one of the monitors in the display and configure it to use Twinview.  You will then need to```
Save to X Configuration File
```and restart xorg (ctrl-alt-backspace).

From here, you can enable compiz-fusion through System : Preferences : Appearance

If you want to configure how compiz runs, apt-get the compizconfig-settings-manager and you can access it through System : Preferences : Advanced Desktop Effects Settings.

Post your findings on this if you could please.

---

### Post by CoolDreamZ on 2007-10-26
This did not work initially for me as there was a problem with my xorg.conf file - a virtual desktop size had been set during installation of Gutsy which was larger than my monitor could actually handle. Once I had fixed my xorg.conf file this procedure worked :-)

I suggest unchecking the "merge with existing file" default when saving the changes in the Nvidia settings manager to your xorg.conf file - _but make sure you have a backup of the original file_

Here is my xorg.conf file
> # nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder26)  Wed Sep 12 14:29:53 PDT 2007

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Sony SDM-HS95P"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 65.0
    VertRefresh     57.0 - 63.0
    ModeLine       "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7950 GT"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 1280x1024@60 +1280+0; DFP-0: 1280x960@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 1280x960@60 +1280+0; DFP-0: 1024x768@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 1024x768@60 +1024+0; DFP-0: 800x600@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 800x600@60 +800+0; DFP-0: 640x480@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 640x480@60 +640+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

### Post by CoolDreamZ on 2007-10-26
Spoke too soon! Because I didn't use the "merge with existing file" when saving the settings my keyboard was reset to default! As I have a British keyboard this caused my key mappings to get messed up :(

So, my advice now is to _use_ the default "merge with existing file" setting when saving your settings in the nvidia configuration manager.

As I had to edit my xorg.conf file to fix the keyboard problem I took the opportunity to tidy it up. Here is the edited version:

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder26)  Wed Sep 12 14:29:53 PDT 2007
# edited as did not use merge option when saving the settings and screwed up keyboard/mouse settings

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Twin Spanned"
    Screen      0  "Screen-L" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor-L"
    VendorName     "Sony"
    ModelName      "Sony SDM-HS95P"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 65.0
    VertRefresh     57.0 - 63.0
    ModeLine       "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7950 GT"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen-L"
    Device         "Videocard"
    Monitor        "Monitor-L"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "DFP-0: 1280x1024@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 1280x1024@60 +1280+0; DFP-0: 1280x960@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 1280x960@60 +1280+0; DFP-0: 1024x768@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 1024x768@60 +1024+0; DFP-0: 800x600@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 800x600@60 +800+0; DFP-0: 640x480@60 +0+0, DFP-1: 640x480@60 +640+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

### Post by superkikim on 2007-10-27
> **RFScheer said:**
> Here's mine.  No editing on the file was needed.  Good luck.  - Robert

Hi Rob,

Thank you so much... I just copy-pasted your xorg.conf to replace mine, and it worked perfectly...

I was trying since last night to get both dual head and extra gnome effect working. And I don't understand why, suddunly it was only working with Xinerama or Twinview, stretching the desktop on both monitor (not really what expected)... I was unable to have again two separated desktops...

However I had to swap my DVI cables to get the correct monitor on the left (didn't want to take the chance to screw it again by playing with "leftto... rightto..... "...

I will save now that damn xorg.conf once for all and keep it safe :-D

EDIT: Damn... I WAS WRONG !!!!

It doesn't work anymore .... My desktop is again stretched across both monitors...

Who knows what parameter where give the information for dual monitor with two desktop, or one large desktop ? is it in the gdm.conf file ? Or where ?!?!? I'm lost... and tired !!!

Hope you can help.

EDIT: OK... I FIND A WAY OUT...

Administration => Screen and Graphics: Configure both screen (secondary extending desktop).

Then, I put in place your xorg.conf file, and it works.....

But it is really a pain to get this working correctly :-( too many config tools, left hand not knowing what right hand does.... Messy and heavy..... :-(

By the way, I can't get Compiz working.... Don't know why....

### Post by CoolDreamZ on 2007-10-28
OK, I think I see what is happening here. The "TwinView" option is a capability of the Nvidia driver that allows the desktop to span more than one monitor. In effect the X Windows system sees only one large display area that is spanned across two monitors by the driver. See these links for further info:

[http://http://www.nvidia.com/object/feature_twinview.html]("http://http://www.nvidia.com/object/feature_twinview.html")

[http://us.download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86_64/100.14.19/README/chapter-13.html]("http://us.download.nvidia.com/XFree86/Linux-x86_64/100.14.19/README/chapter-13.html")

If you want two independent X Window displays you need to configure the system without using the "TwinView" option (this is what I did initially before setting up a TwinView, spanned arrangement). You need two "Device" sections, one for each output from the video card. Type **man xorg.conf** for further details. Here is my xorg.conf file for two separate displays:

```

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

# START OF EDITS
# Chris Thompson 26th October 2007
# wacom stuff removed for clarity
# **********************************************************

#These sections setup a dual-ouput nVidia graphics card
#Primary display output on Video Card (LHS)
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Primary"
	Boardname	"nv"
	Busid		"PCI:2:0:0"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Screen	0
EndSection
#Secondary display output on Video Card (RHS)
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Secondary"
	Boardname	"nv"
	Busid		"PCI:2:0:0"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Screen	1
EndSection

# These sections define the two monitors (both the same model in this case)
# Monitor on LHS
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"L-Monitor"
	Vendorname	"Sony"
	Modelname	"SDM-HS95P"
  	modeline  	"640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  	modeline  	"800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  	modeline  	"1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  	modeline  	"1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
  	modeline  	"1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection
#Monitor on RHS (exactly the same model as LHS)
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"R-Monitor"
	Vendorname	"Sony"
	Modelname	"SDM-HS95P"
  	modeline  	"640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  	modeline  	"800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  	modeline  	"1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  	modeline  	"1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
  	modeline  	"1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

#These sections bind the two Monitors to the Secondary and Primary outputs of the graphics card
#Screen on the LHS
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"L-Screen"
	Device		"Primary"
	Monitor		"L-Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Virtual	1280 1024
		Modes	"1280x1024@60" "1280x960@60" "1024x768@60" "800x600@60"	"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
#Screen on the RHS
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"R-Screen"
	Device		"Secondary"
	Monitor		"R-Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Virtual	1280 1024
		Modes	"1280x1024@60" "1280x960@60" "1024x768@60" "800x600@60"	"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

#This section ties the whole lot together, defining the spatial relationship between the screens
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Screen "L-Screen"
	Screen "R-Screen" RightOf "L-Screen"
EndSection

# END OF EDITS
# *************

Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

```

### Post by SuperMike on 2007-11-15
I have an nVidia GEForce 6200 OC + Gutsy + Dual Screen running just fine. Didn't have to edit a single xorg.conf file. Just booted up, chose Restricted nVidia driver when prompted, rebooted, logged in again, chose Screens and Graphics Administration panel, set proper monitor type on screen 1 (1024x768@59hz) (why 59Hz, I'll never know -- but it switches later to 60hz), set screen 2 to turn on (not disabled) and set it for same monitor type. I also set it to turn on as my left monitor. I then clicked OK, OK, logout, reboot. When I booted back up, my two screens were working fine in dual screen multiple desktop mode. I could move windows between screens and do everything one does in Xinerama mode.

However, I couldn't get Compiz working, but I tried the GUI control panel way, not installing and editing stuff. If one has a way to enable that with a PCI-based nVidia GeForce 6200 OC card with 256MB DDR RAM and Xinerama, please let me see your steps and xorg.conf. Much appreciated.

### Post by bernecky on 2008-04-28
I'm posting this in the hope that others may benefit from
my experience (even though I don't know exactly what I did to get
here...):

I finally got this stuff to, more or less, work. I ended up
deinstalling/removing everything in Ubuntu that smelled like nvidia,
the reinstalling it, as well as the "proprietary nvidia graphics drivers". I had the stretched-across-both-monitors 
stuff at first if I ran gnome, which was an excellent exercise in
the mouse muscles as the mouse had to go from the left end of the left monitor (for apps/places/system) to the right end of the right
monitor (for user switching, workspace switching, etc. 

Recommendation #1: Provide a capability for arranging panel and
taskbar items to the left or right. This may exist, but it is not
obvious from any of the Properties for these.

I found that I had to run "sudo nvidia-setting" from a command prompt
to get it to overwrite /etc/X11/xorg.conf. I set it up as twinview, 
with some struggling, and that's what I'm using now. 

I have attached my xorg.conf as xorg.conf.txt. It was earlier
hand-butchered, so there be some detritus in there...

This config shows up this way:

1. Monitor 0 (LG 226WA) on the right
2. Monitor 1 (Samsung 191T) on the left.
3. Taskbar and panel on Monitor 0.
4. I can move windows from one monitor to the other with the mouse.
5. Maximize does NOT stretch across both monitors (I consider this
   a feature...), but maximizes within a single monitor.

6. I can now enable "assistive technologies", but am not happy with
   the behavior of the "Zoom" facility. My understanding is that
   this is supposed to zoom anything via CTRL-mousewheel, but 
   the zoom behaves erratically:
     e.g., in evolution, it will zoom/unzoom text in the preview
     window, but not in the message list or folder list. I guess the
     idea is that people have poor eyesight only when reading
      messages???
     e.g., in Firefox, it sometimes zooms and other times does not.
     It takes several seconds to do anything, when it does zoom.
     Again, the zooming should, I believe work across the board,
     regardless of application. My eyesight does not change 
     its prescription depending on which window I'm looking at!

7. Running "sudo nvidia-settings" takes several MINUTES before
   the "nvidia server settings" window shows up on the screen.

Early on, I had gnome working in failsafe mode, but it would
not enable compis, nor would the zoom work. In normal (non-failsafe)
mode, it would stretch the window across both monitors. That
behavior went away with this version of xorg.conf and/or nvidia drivers. BTW, I'm using the nvidia drivers from the ubuntu site,
NOT from the nvidia.com web site. I think...


Robert

ps: The lack of any "fuzz" in the mouse cursor position when
trying to grab the edge of a window to grow/shrink it makes
me crazy. It usually takes me a few seconds of trying before
I actually have the edge selected. This is, IMO, a lousy
bit of HCI that should be repaired.

---
